title: redb PostgreSQL and nu_plugin CodeDB relationship decided
owner: David / LifeOS Data Agent / Runtime Kernel
objective: Maintain the explicit owner decision assigning every LifeOS product data class exactly once across PostgreSQL+RuVector, redb, AgentDB, envctl, CodeDB, rtk_nu, and protected secret custody, with directional reconciliation, durability, recovery, and a proof-gated transition path grounded in both immutable expanded architecture anchors.

work_items.decision: canonical durable owner; single writer of truth; RuVector native extension (No-Sidecar)

work_items.surface: transient high-frequency state (thoughts, traces, WAL, dedup cache, geometry math)
work_items.decision: intended transient/pass-through tier; one supervised database opener; complete ordered reconciliation to PostgreSQL through envctl; separate atomic read-only mmap generations and ordered UDS events; never a second durable writer

work_items.surface: nu_plugin CodeDB current durable-on-redb source blobs
work_items.decision: transitional; migrate per class to PostgreSQL under one SHA-256 identity using codedb_store_pg::PgStore + blobstore_parity.rs; only after a green live parity run
work_items.restore: verified swap under identical content identity; no data loss

work_items.decision: non-authoritative for shared macro-state; witnessed by SHAKE256 chains rooted in PostgreSQL

work_items.surface: derived projections (materialized dirs for musl builds)
work_items.decision: derived and disposable; re-projected deterministically from PostgreSQL via module_path

work_items.surface: protected secret bytes, policies, grants, leases, custody, and audit
work_items.owner: PostgreSQL + RuVector under the six-part secret lifecycle
work_items.decision: encrypted bytes and complete custody history are durable; plaintext exists only at an authorized lease-bound target and never in redb, logs, UI, model context, proof, or Git
work_items.restore: PostgreSQL PITR plus owner-controlled root material and tested custody recovery

work_items.surface: repository, task, context, refactor, format, consolidation, upgrade, and multi-merge operations after cutover
work_items.owner: PostgreSQL + RuVector operational coordinator and complete record
work_items.decision: Git, Meta, GitKB, ICM, runners, and editors remain governed physical executors or projections; they do not become parallel durable planning authorities
work_items.restore: database job/lease/witness record plus exact Git and artifact receipts

corrects: generated/store_001_decision_brief.md and pre-anchor keyring/envctl assumptions (see store_relationship_decision.json and 1.0_VISION/Architecture_Anchors/anchor_conflict_ledger.csv)
